senses.definition: to treat someone in authority with high respect, particularly in order to get their approval

examples.x: The sycophantic assistant would always bow and scrape whenever the boss entered the room, hoping to gain favor.
The politician's supporters lined up outside his office, ready to bow and scrape for a chance to meet him and seek his endorsement.
The salesperson's attempts to bow and scrape to the demanding customer only resulted in further frustration and dissatisfaction.
The aristocracy of the time expected servants to bow and scrape, reinforcing the rigid social hierarchy and class divisions.
The old-fashioned tradition of bowing and scraping in certain formal ceremonies has largely faded away in modern society.

senses.definition: to try to get something from a person by showing insincere kindness to them

examples.x: The corrupt politician surrounded himself with sycophants who were always ready to kiss his ass.
The arrogant celebrity demanded that the hotel staff kiss his ass, displaying a sense of entitlement and superiority.

senses.definition: to get people to praise one, often by pretending not to be happy with oneself

examples.x: Sarah knew she looked stunning in her new dress, so she subtly mentioned it to her friends, fishing for a compliment.
When John showed off his cooking skills, he kept glancing at his guests, hoping they would fish for a compliment about the delicious meal.
The actor pretended to be modest about their performance, hoping others would fish for a compliment and praise their talent.

senses.definition: used to describe someone who never puts their word into action

examples.x: In the end they seem to have been all mouth and no trousers.
Very few carry out these threats and those that do not, lose some credibility with their children who see them as all mouth and no action.
She claimed she would confront the bully, but in the end, she proved to be all mouth and backed down when confronted.
The politician made grand promises during the campaign, but once elected, he proved to be all mouth and no substance.
Despite his claims of being a skilled athlete, he was all mouth and no action when it came to participating in sports.

senses.definition: to try to please or get closer to someone for one's personal gain

examples.x: The dictator surrounded himself with loyal followers who would lick his boots at his command.
The corrupt official expected everyone to lick his boots in exchange for favors and privileges.
